name: castAnalysis
invariants: output.result.cast === undefined || output.result.cast.every(c => Number.isFinite(c.totalCost) && c.totalCost >= 0 && Number.isFinite(c.dailyRate) && Number.isFinite(c.scenes))
_note: film-studios.castAnalysis is a PURE per-cast cost calculator (LENS_ACTIONS / domains/filmstudios.js, input under artifact.data, reads data.cast[]). totalCost = sceneCount * dailyRate / 3, summed to totalCastBudget. No DB / no money moved. FAIL-CLOSED (landed 2026-06-27): the prior `parseFloat(c.dailyRate) || 0` passed a poisoned dailyRate (Infinity / 1e400→Infinity) through, minting a non-finite totalCost / totalCastBudget. The handler now clamps each numeric leaf to finite + non-negative (`Number.isFinite(n) && n > 0 ? n : 0`) before the math, so every cost stays finite. Per-cast numerics live INSIDE the cast array (not top-level numeric params) and the clamp returns ok:true with 0 (graceful degrade, not reject), so cast is declared `array` — not a V2 ok_true_on_poisoned_number defect. Empty cast degrades to { message: 'Add cast …' }.
